summary: Fresh adversarial UX re-audit of the Ten80Ten Content Engine after the c09439b QA pass. The board, modals, empty/loading/error states and most viewport handling are solid (h-dvh, true-center modals, real empty states, KeyboardSensor for drag-drop). Remaining defects: three user-facing 'Pipeline' branding leaks (kanban tab, repurpose button, PWA manifest) that violate AGENTS.md section 6, one mobile viewport regression (request-access page uses min-h-screen), the kanban card drag handle is invisible on touch devices and far below the 44px tap-target floor, and the @mention dropdown has no keyboard navigation. No focus traps in any of the bespoke modals.

findings.title: Kanban card drag handle is invisible on touch devices and ~20px (far below 44px tap target)
findings.description: The explicit drag handle button is styled 'opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100'. Touch devices have no hover state, so the handle never appears for mobile/tablet users — the documented keyboard/handle drag affordance is effectively desktop-only. Even when visible it is p-1 around a w-3 h-3 icon (~20x20px), far under the owner's 44px minimum tap target. Mobile users can still long-press the card body via TouchSensor (delay 150), but the intended discoverable handle is unreachable, and the only visible drag affordance fails the hard tap-target rule.
findings.reproducer: Open the Content Engine board on a 375px touch viewport. The grip icon on each card never renders (no hover). On desktop, hover a card and try to hit the 20px grip.
findings.recommendedFix: Make the handle always visible on touch (e.g. 'opacity-100 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100') and enlarge the hit area to at least 44x44px (increase padding or add an invisible expanded touch target) while keeping the icon visually small.

findings.title: User-facing 'Pipeline' branding leaks violate AGENTS.md section 6 (Content Engine, not Pipeline)
findings.description: AGENTS.md section 6 mandates the product is called 'Content Engine' in all user-facing text; 'Pipeline' is internal-only. The board's primary view tab button renders the literal label 'Pipeline' (kanban-board.tsx line 233). repurpose-modal.tsx line 242 shows a button labelled 'Send to Pipeline'. public/manifest.json line 4 description reads 'Content Pipeline & Social Media Management' — this is the PWA install description shown on phones and desktops. layout.tsx already correctly says 'Content Engine', so the manifest is an incomplete prior fix. dashboard-page.tsx also labels a card 'Pipeline Funnel' and a link 'View full pipeline' (lines 167, 272). All are user-visible.
findings.reproducer: Open the board: the left tab reads 'Pipeline'. Archive a post and click Repurpose: the CTA reads 'Send to Pipeline'. Install the PWA: the description says 'Content Pipeline'.
findings.recommendedFix: Rename the kanban view tab to 'Board' or 'Content Engine'; change the repurpose CTA to 'Send to Content Engine' (or 'Send to Ideas'); update manifest.json description to 'Content Engine & Social Media Management for Ten80Ten' to match layout.tsx; rename 'Pipeline Funnel' and 'View full pipeline' on the dashboard.

findings.title: request-access page uses min-h-screen instead of min-h-dvh (mobile viewport regression)
findings.description: Both the success state (line 55) and the form state (line 73) of the public Request Access page use 'min-h-screen'. The owner's hard mobile rule requires 'h-dvh / min-h-dvh' so iOS Safari's dynamic toolbar does not cause a layout jump or cut-off footer. Every other auth page (auth/setup, auth/reset-password, auth/forgot-password) and the app shell correctly use min-h-dvh / h-dvh, so this page is the lone regression. Request Access is a public, frequently mobile-accessed page.
findings.reproducer: Open /request-access on an iPhone in Safari. The 100vh-based layout includes the URL bar height, pushing content below the visible fold and causing a scroll jump when the toolbar collapses.
findings.recommendedFix: Replace 'min-h-screen' with 'min-h-dvh' on both div elements (lines 55 and 73).

findings.title: @mention dropdown has no keyboard navigation, no Escape, and is mouse-only
findings.description: The mention autocomplete dropdown opens on '@' and lists active team members, but it is fully mouse-driven: no ArrowUp/ArrowDown to move a highlight, no Enter to select, no Escape to dismiss. A keyboard-only user typing a caption or revision note cannot pick a mention without reaching for the mouse. The dropdown also renders 'bottom-full' (above the textarea); for the caption field near the top of the scrollable Create Post form this can be clipped by the modal header.
findings.reproducer: In Create Post, focus the caption, type '@', then try to select a teammate using only the keyboard. Nothing responds to arrows or Enter.
findings.recommendedFix: Add keyboard handlers: ArrowUp/ArrowDown to move an active index, Enter/Tab to insert the highlighted member, Escape to close. Add role=listbox/option and aria-activedescendant. Optionally flip the dropdown to open downward when there is no space above.

findings.title: Bespoke modals have no focus trap — Tab escapes to background page
findings.description: CreatePostModal, RevisionModal, KickbackModal, RepurposeModal, MediaPicker, AvatarCropModal and the asset-review drawer all handle Escape-to-close but none trap focus inside the dialog. Pressing Tab repeatedly moves the focus ring onto the obscured background page (sidebar nav, board cards), and none set role=dialog/aria-modal on the bespoke ones (only ValidationErrorModal and the delete/lightbox dialogs do). Keyboard and screen-reader users lose their place and can operate hidden controls. ValidationErrorModal is the one good example — it focuses its dismiss button and sets role=alertdialog.
findings.reproducer: Open Create Post, press Tab past the last field (Create Post button). Focus jumps to the sidebar behind the dim overlay instead of cycling back to the modal's first control.
findings.recommendedFix: Add a shared focus-trap (cycle Tab/Shift+Tab within the dialog, focus the first interactive element on open, restore focus to the trigger on close) and add role=dialog + aria-modal=true + aria-labelledby to each bespoke modal root.

findings.title: RevisionModal submit button is not disabled during submit — double-submit risk
findings.description: handleSubmit calls submitReapproval then immediately addToast + setNote(''), with no in-flight state. The Submit button is only disabled by '!isValid' (note length >= 10). Between the click and pendingReapproval clearing, a fast double-tap (common on mobile) can fire submitReapproval twice for the same card. KickbackModal correctly guards this with an 'uploading' state that disables the button ('disabled={!isValid || uploading}'); RevisionModal has no equivalent. The same double-submit gap exists in RepurposeModal handleSubmit (lines 46-84) — createCard can fire twice before onClose.
findings.reproducer: Open Submit for Re-Approval, type a valid note, double-click Submit Revision quickly. Two re-approval submissions / audit events can be created.
findings.recommendedFix: Add a 'submitting' state to RevisionModal and RepurposeModal, set it true at the start of handleSubmit, disable the primary button while true, and reset on completion — mirror the KickbackModal pattern.

findings.title: Kanban board horizontal scroll is the only way to reach later columns on mobile
findings.description: On mobile the five-column board is a horizontal scroll container ('flex gap-3 overflow-x-auto snap-x snap-mandatory'). Each PipelineColumn is min-w-[240px] (pipeline-column.tsx line 20), so at 375px only one column is visible at a time and the user must horizontally swipe through all five to reach 'Posted'. There is no column picker, no tab strip, and no scroll affordance/arrow. Combined with the touch drag (long-press on card body), moving a card across columns on a phone is awkward — the user must drag while the target column is off-screen. snap-mandatory at least makes paging predictable, but discoverability of columns 3-5 is poor.
findings.reproducer: Open the board at 375px. Only the Ideas column shows. There is no visible hint that four more columns exist to the right.
findings.recommendedFix: Add a lightweight column tab/segmented control above the board on mobile (md:hidden) that scrolls the chosen column into view, or show partial peek of the next column plus dot indicators. Low-risk: purely additive UI.

findings.title: Dashboard auto-fit scale transform can clip content and create a dead horizontal scroll region
findings.description: On screens >= 640px the dashboard measures its inner height and applies 'transform: scale(s)' with 'width: 100/s%' to fit everything without scrolling. On short laptop/tablet windows this shrinks all text and controls (sub-1.0 scale) so numbers and labels become hard to read, and the widened inner element ('width: 100/scale%') can create a horizontal overflow region inside the otherwise sm:overflow-hidden container. ResizeObserver-driven scale also causes a visible re-layout flash on window resize. The mobile path (innerWidth < 640) correctly opts out.
findings.reproducer: Open the dashboard on a 1280x640 window. The whole dashboard shrinks; tap targets and numbers scale below comfortable size and a faint horizontal scrollbar can appear.
findings.recommendedFix: Set a minimum scale floor (e.g. Math.max(s, 0.8)) and below that allow natural vertical scrolling instead of shrinking further; or drop the scale transform entirely and let the dashboard scroll on short viewports the way mobile already does.

findings.title: Media Library deletes have no confirmation — one tap permanently removes assets
findings.description: deleteSelected immediately removes the selected media rows from state and issues a Supabase delete, with no confirmation dialog and no toast/undo. A single tap on the 'Delete' text button in the toolbar (after selecting assets) destroys them. Contrast with the post delete flow in asset-review-drawer, which has a proper 'Delete this post?' alertdialog. Media assets can be referenced by posts; an accidental bulk delete is silent and unrecoverable from the UI.
findings.reproducer: On the Media Library, select one or more files, tap Delete in the toolbar. Files vanish instantly with no confirm and no way to undo.
findings.recommendedFix: Add a confirmation dialog before deleteSelected runs (reuse the alertdialog pattern from asset-review-drawer), showing how many files and whether any are in use. At minimum show a toast with the count after deletion.

findings.title: Literal em dash rendered as the empty-aspect placeholder in Creator Studio
findings.description: The Aspect read-only field renders the literal character '—' (em dash) when no aspect is resolved: '{resolved ? formatAspectChip(resolved) : "—"}'. The owner's copy rules ban em dashes in user-facing text. presence-label.tsx line 76 returns the same '—' as a fallback label. These are the em dashes that actually reach the rendered DOM (most other em dashes in the codebase are in code comments).
findings.reproducer: Open Creator Studio, add a row, leave media/format/platforms blank. The Aspect chip shows a bare em dash.
findings.recommendedFix: Replace the '—' placeholder with a hyphen-minus '-' or a neutral word like 'Not set' in studio-page.tsx line 720 and presence-label.tsx line 76.

findings.title: Toast micro-copy uses em dashes throughout — violates the no-em-dash copy rule
findings.description: Many toast messages are written with em dashes, e.g. 'Revision requested — creator and approvers notified.' (kickback-modal.tsx line 69), 'Revision submitted — sent for re-approval.' (revision-modal.tsx line 31), 'Link copied — share with your team' (media-picker.tsx line 140), and several in settings-page.tsx and studio-page.tsx. Toasts are user-facing copy, and the owner's writing rules ban em dashes (use commas, periods, or semicolons).
findings.reproducer: Trigger any revision/kickback/upload action and read the toast — it contains an em dash.
findings.recommendedFix: Replace em dashes in all addToast strings with a period or comma, e.g. 'Revision requested. Creator and approvers notified.' This is a low-risk find-and-replace across the toast call sites.

findings.title: Media Library list view forces a 700px-wide horizontal scroll on mobile instead of a card stack
findings.description: The list view wraps a fixed 'min-w-[700px]' grid in an overflow-x-auto container. On a 375px phone the user must horizontally scroll a six-column table (File / Type / Folder / Status / Added By / Timestamp) to read each row. The owner's hard mobile rule states tables should become card-stacks on mobile. The grid view (cards) is available, but list is a valid user choice and degrades badly. The view toggle itself is hidden on mobile ('hidden sm:flex', line 305), so a user who last selected list on desktop is stuck with the 700px table on their phone with no way to switch back to grid.
findings.reproducer: On desktop set Media Library to List view, then open on a phone. You get a 700px-wide scrolling table and the grid/list toggle is hidden so you cannot switch back.
findings.recommendedFix: Render a stacked card layout for the list view below the sm breakpoint, or force grid view on mobile, or keep the view toggle visible on mobile so users can recover.

findings.title: Settings 'Week starts on' and several toggles are non-functional controls with no feedback
findings.description: The 'Week starts on' select (lines 424-428) has no value/onChange wired — it renders Monday/Sunday options that do nothing. The Publishing and Notifications sections use a <Toggle /> component with only a defaultOn prop and no persistence, so flipping 'Auto-publish', 'Analytics tracking', 'Email notifications' etc. changes nothing and is not saved. A user toggling these believes they have configured the workspace. Connected Accounts 'Connect' buttons and several 'Manage' buttons just toast 'coming soon', which is at least honest, but the toggles silently lie.
findings.reproducer: Open Settings > General, flip 'Auto-publish' off, navigate away and back — it resets. Change 'Week starts on' to Sunday — the calendar still starts on Sunday-index regardless.
findings.recommendedFix: Either wire these controls to real persisted settings, or disable them with a 'coming soon' affordance consistent with the Connect/Manage buttons so users are not misled into thinking a setting was saved.

findings.title: AvatarCropModal Apply button never re-enables if cropping fails — user can get stuck
findings.description: handleSave sets saving=true but never sets it back to false. On the success path the parent unmounts the modal so it does not matter, but the catch fallback (fetch original image, onCropComplete) also leaves saving=true. If onCropComplete's downstream upload throws or the parent keeps the modal mounted, the Apply Crop button stays disabled and shows 'Cropping...' forever with no error surfaced to the user. There is also no toast on the crop-failure fallback path, so a degraded result is silent.
findings.reproducer: Force getCroppedImg to throw (e.g. a tainted cross-origin image). The fallback runs but the button stays stuck on 'Cropping...'.
findings.recommendedFix: Wrap handleSave in try/finally and reset saving=false in the finally block; surface a toast if the crop fell back to the uncropped original.
